rank_rationale: #1 over #2: three steepness settings and two stone sizes create richer rule-based paths than flat budget stones, which fits the nearly-three sequenced-movement brief better.
include_rationale: At nearly three, movement gets planned: step, pause, turn, rather than random crashing around the room. These stones let you invent little living-room routes with clear rules, so balance practice becomes a game you build together. That is why they suit step, pause and turn paths so well for this age.

rank_rationale: #2 over #3: much lower spend than Edx stumps while still clearing the 34, 36m age gate with 18m+, making it the honest budget path before premium or specialist kit.
include_rationale: Not every family wants premium stones on day one. Five textured pieces are enough for a short hallway route with pause points, so you can see whether path play sticks before spending more. That kinder first look is why this suits smaller budgets without cutting the balance skill short.

rank_rationale: #3 over #4: nesting carry-bag storage and ribbed sensory tops solve small-flat clutter better than three tall hilltops that eat floor space.
include_rationale: Hallways and small flats need kit that packs away. Nesting stump sizes still give a proper step-up path, then collapse into one stack with a carry bag so the living room is free again. That tidy pack away is why they suit small spaces while balance practice stays part of everyday play.

rating_source: Liberty House Toys shows 1 verified review on check date
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: fixed height progression adds step-up/step-down sequencing that low beam sections cannot, while staying inside the not-daredevil brief.
include_rationale: Once flat paths are sorted, a little height makes the next step interesting without turning play into daredevil jumping. Three fixed levels invite step-up, pause and step-down, which matches the planned movement this age is growing into. That is why they suit height practice beside flatter stones you may already own.

rank_rationale: #5: rope gaps add distance judgement without a second Step-A beam SKU that truncates into the same title as nesting stumps.
include_rationale: Distance judgement grows fast around this age: can they stretch to the next stone without rushing? Rope-linked gaps make that stretch visible and adjustable, so you can widen the challenge as confidence grows. That controlled reach is why they suit gap practice better than another set of freestanding stones.
